title: Sodium-ion and 3D-printed battery opportunity
why_it_matters: Battery chemistry and form-factor innovation connects Ω-BAT-T, Ω-ENERGY-T, MEMS, drones, wearables, and low-voltage prototype systems.
actionable_opportunity: Build an OAK comparison matrix for Li-ion, sodium-ion, solid-state, and printed batteries across cost, safety, cycle life, Wh/kg, manufacturability, and prototype relevance.

oak_check.risk: Battery breakthrough claims often fail on cycle life, scale-up, temperature range, safety, or realistic cost assumptions.
oak_check.limit: Treat as a screening and comparison opportunity, not proof of superior battery technology.
oak_check.falsification_route: Collect primary datasheets or papers and reject any candidate lacking cycle-life, energy-density, safety, and scaling evidence.
oak_check.m_minus_warning: No battery claim should be promoted without cost, cycle, safety, and scaling checks.

sources.title: Source verification required: sodium-ion and printed-battery item mentioned in chat briefing

next_action: Create a battery OAK matrix template and verify the primary sodium-ion / printed-battery sources.
business_funding_signal: Could support energy-storage consulting, prototype selection, grants, or prior-art tracking.
